zh.lifeSummary: 當William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche於1276年12月18日出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯時，他的父親Eudes la Zouche69歲，母親Millicent de Cantilupe31歲。他於1296年2月15日之前與Maud Lovell在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1301年登記服兵役。他去世於1351年3月12日在他的家鄉享年74歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche于1276年12月18日出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯时，他的父亲Eudes la Zouche69岁，母亲Millicent de Cantilupe31岁。他于1296年2月15日之前与Maud Lovell在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1301年登记服兵役。他去世于1351年3月12日在他的家乡享年74岁，埋葬在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Zouche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Zaucha
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Casimir, Franciszek, Kazimierz, Krzysztof, Mieczyslaw, Rafal, Wojciech.
Polish: descriptive nickname from za uchem ‘behind the ear’, denoting someone who had something behind one of his ears. Compare Zoucha .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche was born on 18 December 1276, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Eudes la Zouche, was 69 and his mother, Millicent de Cantilupe, was 31. He married Maud Lovell before 15 February 1296, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. He registered for military service in 1301. He died on 12 March 1351, in his hometown, at the age of 74, and was buried in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche est né le 18 décembre 1276, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Eudes la Zouche, avait 69 ans et sa mère, Millicent de Cantilupe, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Maud Lovell avant 15 février 1296, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1301. Il est décédé le 12 mars 1351, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche am 18. Dezember 1276 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Eudes la Zouche 69 und seine Mutter Millicent de Cantilupe 31. Er hat vor 15. Februar 1296 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England Maud Lovell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er hat sich 1301 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 12. März 1351 im Alter von 74 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche è nato il il 18 dicembre 1276 nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Eudes la Zouche, aveva 69 anni e il suo madre, Millicent de Cantilupe, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Maud Lovell prima del 15 febbraio 1296, nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1301. Morì il 12 marzo 1351, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William la Zouche 1st Baron Zoucheが1276年12月18日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで生まれた時，父Eudes la Zoucheは69歳，母Millicent de Cantilupeは31歳であった。1296年2月15日以前にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスでMaud Lovellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1301年に兵役登録をした。彼は1351年3月12日に故郷で74歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 애쉬비 데 라 즈우치
ko.lifeSummary: William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche(이)가 1276년 12월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Eudes la Zouche(은)는 69세였고, 그의 어머니 Millicent de Cantilupe(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1296년 2월 15일 이전 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 Maud Lovell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1301년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1351년 3월 12일 그의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche nasceu em 18 de dezembro de 1276, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Eudes la Zouche, tinha 69 anos e sua mãe, Millicent de Cantilupe, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Lovell antes 15 de fevereiro de 1296, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1301. Ele faleceu em 12 de março de 1351, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche родился 18 декабря 1276 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Eudes la Zouche было 69 лет, а матери, которую звали Millicent de Cantilupe, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Lovell перед 15 февраль 1296 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. В 1301 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 12 марта 1351 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.18 Dec 1276–12 March 1351: 18 de diciembre de 1276–12 de marzo de 1351
es.Richards Castle, Ludlow, Herefordshire,: Richard's Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche nació el 18 de diciembre de 1276, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Eudes la Zouche, tenía 69 años y su mamá, Millicent de Cantilupe, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Maud Lovell antes de 15 de febrero de 1296, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1301. Murió el 12 de marzo de 1351, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
